What's going everyone? Welcome back. Core habit number three. Full responsibility always as in take full responsibility always. So here's the theory behind this. This is one of the habits that will make you the most money. I mean all the habits will. But this one especially is just locus of control. Who controls your life? And so there's two main rules to taking absolute extreme ownership. So this is from Jacka Willing, these first two bullets. But I like full responsibility just because I feel like it resonates. So rule number one, there are no excuses. If something did not happen, the only excuse is irrelevant. And I mean that genuinely and you probably heard this before but like do you live it? Do you live that way? Do you blame your marker place? Do you blame your trainers? Do you blame the weather? Like what reason do you have? And whatever that reason is that you believe that you are not successful, that is an excuse. And I try not to have this as a tough talking thing because like this is not my style. But being very real, if you blame anyone but yourself, you are inherently becoming a victim and choosing to be a victim and choosing to give your power away. So rule number one, there are no excuses. Rule number two, everything is always your fault. So that's not his rule number two but that's my rule number two. Everything is always your fault. I think he says there's no one else to blame but essentially the same thing. Everything is your fault always. If a trainer shows up late at 5 am, who's fault is it? Yours. If you go out of town and the gym's not clean, who's fault is it? Yours. If your marketing doesn't work, who's fault is it? Your fault. If a competitor comes into town and half your gym goes away, who's fault is it? Yours. Because as soon as you start thinking that way, then you can only think in solutions and you're thinking gaps and deficits of what skill did I lack that if I had that skill, I would have prevented this. If your marketing isn't working, you need to be better at marketing. If the competitor down the street comes and takes half your gym, which never actually happens, you're like, if that were to happen, then it means that your fulfillment sucks and that you had no loyalty, you had no brand. People are competing on price, which means that the lowest level of competition and value you're providing. So that's your deficit. You're not good at providing value. You're not good at creating a culture. You're not good at creating relationships and so people didn't value you. It's not the gym's fault. The gym exposed your weakness. These circumstances expose your weaknesses. They are not the reason for your failures. If that can sink in, you will be so much more profitable in life. The thing here is that when you take full responsibility, your actions are the only thing that we can actually control. If we blame other things and we're saying, cool, I don't need to try anymore, and that doesn't change our circumstances. That doesn't change our outcomes by saying, well, oops, it doesn't change anything. The only thing that you can do if you are not where you want to be, it is your fault, but you were the only one who can fix it. You have to think that way. You have to think that way. I'm telling you, you have to take ownership. If your marriage isn't what you want it to be, it is your fault. It's not their fault. It is your fault because there's deficit that you can make up. The nice thing is, like I said, when you take 100% responsibility, you get out of victimized and you only see solutions because it's the only thing you can see is actions. What's my deficit and what can I do to fix it? That's it. Whenever you start blaming other things else, then you no longer take accountability, which means you can't fix it because it's not under your control, which means you're fucked. I'm just going to sit here and be a victim of my circumstances, which are unchanging and I accept that I will continue to lose. That's really what you're saying. Don't think like that. This is a habit from a mindset standpoint of how you approach and how you see the world. This is one of my favorite quotes. I don't know who said it first, but we are all self-made, only millionaires and billionaires own it. You are self-made. We are all self-made. Right now, if you are a drinking problem or you drink too much or you're not consistent and your relationship is messy and the gym doesn't grow or whatever it is, your business doesn't grow, you are self-made. You have made that. That is your creation and it is a mirror reflection of you. You can either blame or you can take ownership and change it. That's the theory. Now, here's tactics for how you can take full responsibility. Personnel, there are no bad teams, only bad leaders. If your team does not perform, it is because you are not a good leader. You hired them, you trained them, you managed them. What do you do here? You go through the leadership portal and you implement every single thing that Lele laid out. The leadership portal will give you how to run your team like a well-oiled machine, how to do your one-on-ones, how to do team meetings, how to do huddles, the structure for every single position, how you communicate that, how you communicate expectations. When something does something wrong, when someone does something good, all of those things are trainable. You're not boring great leaders, you become one. Don't feed it to them, I'm not a good leader. No one is when they start. You'll get better. Anything that has to do with personnel is you because you don't know how to lead. Taxing number two here. Watch all the videos. You are responsible for this. If you don't have time, that is your responsibility. That is the decision you made to prioritize other things. If you're like, I haven't gone through all this stuff yet, I'm busy. Whatever it is, it's your fault. Make time. That's what the consistency number one was. You have to make time and it is 100% on you, on us, to own our circumstances. Own them, follow through, and execute. Once you watch them, do what is inside of them. No one can do push-ups for you or eat the food for you. We talk to our clients like this all the time. It's like, I can give you all the meal plans, I can give you all the workouts, but you have to do them. We can give you the cadence of how to run your team. We can give you the org structure. We can give you the marketing. We can give you the sales. We can give you the recordings. We can give you the meal plans, grocery lists, all the things that you would ever need to crush this. We have enough seven-figure gyms to paint our walls with. The process is work. It's just you need to do them. If you aren't doing them, then it is your fault and it is your responsibility to figure out how you can. I'm going to say it again, go through the leadership training. Especially for most people, their teams are not operating the way they need to be in order to run their business. The leadership portal will give you the first step to how to actually run your team like a well-owned machine so that you can actually step away and have some of that freedom that you want. It starts there. It starts with the culture and the culture starts with you. That's the leadership training. That's the full responsibility tactics. I don't want this one to sound hard, like hardcore. I'm not a hardcore person. It's just that there's nothing that turns me off more than someone who's a victim, someone who complains. It's just like a complaining does not get you anywhere. You don't make progress with that and you literally give your power away. You give it to whatever you're pointing at. I blame my mom because she didn't love me enough. I blame my dad. I blame my reading disability. I blame my education. I blame my upbringing. I blame my whatever. You're just giving the power to that thing. So take it back.
